roleDescriptions.pw: Writes original contest problems for all divisions, checks them for accuracy and fairness, and puts together complete solution sets. Problems go through internal review and testing before the competition.
roleDescriptions.ops: Takes care of the practical side of running the contest, such as rooms, timing, materials, registration check-in, and day-of coordination. Also handles sponsor outreach and serves as the main point of contact when issues come up during the event.
roleDescriptions.pr: Promotes the competition to schools, clubs, and the broader community through announcements, social media, and outreach materials. Keeps branding consistent across platforms and works with Web Dev to keep the website visuals up to date.
roleDescriptions.webdev: Builds and maintains the competition website, including event info, registration, and submission systems. Makes sure everything is accessible, clear, and works on mobile. Coordinates with PR on branding and with Operations on logistics updates.
roleDescriptions.activities: Plans additional functions on the competition day that make the event more welcoming, enriching, and enjoyable for all competitors.

members.name: Benjamin Zhang
members.bio: Benjamin is a junior at Lynbrook High School who is fascinated by mathematics, computer science, and cryptography. Outside of his extracurriculars, he can be found playing his oboe or chatting with his friends. As LMO's President this year, Benjamin hopes to inspire the next generation of mathematicians and scientists in a fun and engaging way!

members.name: Yunfei Xia
members.bio: Yunfei, also known as Steven, is an Olympiad Geometry grinder at Lynbrook, especially in favor of projective. He is one of the only few who belong to the intersection of Track and Field and Math, after many quit. He liked student-organized math tournaments and was motivated to initiate LMO.
